 {"id":"bd-28h","title":"ext4: Implement CRC32C checksum verification (superblock + inode + dir tail)","description":"Goal: verify ext4 metadata checksums when metadata_csum feature is enabled.

Deliverables:
- Add crc32c dependency (or equivalent) in appropriate crate.
- Implement verify_superblock_checksum() (note: superblock checksum rules differ from group desc).
- Implement inode checksum verification hook (phased).
- Implement directory block tail checksum verification hook (phased).

Acceptance:
- Unit tests: checksum passes on known-good fixture bytes, fails on flipped bit.
- Scrub pipeline can reuse these hooks to detect corruption.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:52.406901062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:32.714655569Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:32.714637305Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"]}


This is where we reach meaningful parity beyond 'can parse a superblock'.\n\nAcceptance: can resolve paths, read directory entries, read file data (extents), and report metadata via harness; later phases add writes + journal/MVCC integration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:13.171667672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:02.965848077Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:02.965826407Z","close_reason":"Read-only acceptance criteria met (lookup/readdir/read/xattr/htree/symlink). Write path deferred to bd-zge.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29o","depends_on_id":"bd-16k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:06.665935833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29o","depends_on_id":"bd-23s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:06.747444684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29o","depends_on_id":"bd-zge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:06.832473890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-29o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where parsing turns into an actual filesystem.

We split into:
- ext4 Read-Only MVP (lookup/readdir/read) to unlock FUSE mounting and meaningful parity.
- ext4 Advanced (htree, symlink, xattr, etc).
- Write Path epic (alloc/journal/MVCC integration) for later.

All semantics work must be fixture-backed and routed through a minimal FsOps trait.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29uwc","title":"port ext4 reserved GDT online-resize parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:26:18.007838506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T09:03:06.174851340Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T09:03:06.172757508Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 online-resize reserved-GDT parity with RESIZE_INODE and group-add planning","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-29z","title":"EPIC: btrfs Write Path","description":"# EPIC: btrfs Write Path

## PURPOSE
Enable write operations on btrfs filesystem images through FUSE, completing the dual-filesystem write capability.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gaps:
- btrfs transaction/delayed-ref/scrub parity

btrfs write path requires:
1. Transaction boundaries (btrfs native transactions + FrankenFS MVCC)
2. Delayed reference counting (extent back-references)
3. COW tree updates (metadata + data)
4. Checksum tree updates
5. Free space tracking
6. FUSE write surface integration

## CURRENT STATE (CODE)
- btrfs read path works for basic images
- Tree walking implemented in ffs-btrfs
- Superblock/header parsing complete
- No write-path primitives exist

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Implement btrfs transaction model compatible with MVCC
2. Implement delayed reference tracking
3. Implement COW B-tree mutation (insert/delete/update)
4. Wire write ops via FsOps and FUSE

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `echo "test" > /mnt/btrfs/newfile.txt` works on btrfs image
2. Changes persist after unmount/remount
3. COW semantics preserved (no in-place overwrites)
4. MVCC conflict detection works for btrfs

## V1 SCOPE LIMITS
- Single-device images only
- No subvolumes initially
- No compression/encryption
- No RAID profiles

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E btrfs RW test (bd-2ju) passes
3. Extent allocation, delayed refs, and transaction model functional
4. COW writes produce correct version chains
5. btrfs write path integrated with MVCC","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:33.304814068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:32.044887960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:32.044873263Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuse","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":96,"issue_id":"bd-29z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-29z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: format_path
Mapped graveyard sections: 8.16 SSI certification + 5.1 Typestate + 0.15 Tail decomposition
EV score: 10.666666666666666 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: txn_retry_budget=3, read_latency_p99_budget_ms=50
Fallback trigger: read-only mode + strict compatibility checks

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-b5on","title":"Implement ext4 indirect block addressing and ZERO_RANGE fixes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:02.268634162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:16.655433191Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:16.654585948Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 indirect block addressing:
(1) resolve_indirect_block(): resolves logical blocks via 12 direct + 1 indirect + 1 double-indirect + 1 triple-indirect pointers in i_block[0..14]
(2) read_ext4_indirect(): reads file data block-by-block using indirect resolver
(3) Wired into FsOps::read() ext4 path: checks EXT4_EXTENTS_FL; if not set, uses indirect addressing
(4) Removed EXTENTS from REQUIRED_V1 incompat set — both extents and indirect now supported
Also fixed 3 bugs in review: compressed extent size overflow, decompressed size validation, inline data xattr parsing.
473 ffs-core tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","indirect","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-b80","title":"Repair: Implement scrub pipeline (detect corruption signals)","description":"Goal: detect corrupted blocks/metadata reliably.

Deliverables:
- Define signals:
  - ext4 metadata_csum / btrfs csum verification failures
  - structural invariant violations (bounds, tree ordering)
- Implement ScrubReport with per-block findings and severity.

- Harness can inject corruption (flip bit) and scrub reports it deterministically.
- Scrub does not panic on corrupted data; it returns structured findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:28.702412790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.268015147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.267938143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-25k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.916224379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.995317684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-bejk","title":"Fix dx_root test layout, read-directory EISDIR check, and close stale clippy bead","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:41.502809158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.140304578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.139616074Z","close_reason":"Fixed 3 issues:

-{"id":"bd-dkms6","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_image fixture no longer stamps root-tree tree_block_csum","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_image()` is still constructing a synthetic btrfs root-tree leaf without stamping its `tree_block_csum` field.

## Evidence
- Builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22119-22204`
- It writes a leaf header and item payload at `root_logical = 0x4000`, but never calls the existing test CRC helper used elsewhere (`stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c` at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22283-22287`).
- The workspace test rerun still fails all `build_btrfs_image()` consumers with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`:
  - `open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`

## Why this is high severity
This builder underpins the basic btrfs open/root-tree smoke tests. Right now those tests are red because the fixture no longer satisfies the parser’s checksum contract.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Stamp the synthetic root-tree block checksum after finalizing the leaf contents, and confirm the minimal open/root-tree tests go green independently of the larger readdir fixture family.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:27.513003227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:27.513003227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
+{"id":"bd-dkms6","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_image fixture no longer stamps root-tree tree_block_csum","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_image()` is still constructing a synthetic btrfs root-tree leaf without stamping its `tree_block_csum` field.

## Evidence
- Builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22119-22204`
- It writes a leaf header and item payload at `root_logical = 0x4000`, but never calls the existing test CRC helper used elsewhere (`stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c` at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22283-22287`).
- The workspace test rerun still fails all `build_btrfs_image()` consumers with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`:
  - `open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
  - `open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`

## Why this is high severity
This builder underpins the basic btrfs open/root-tree smoke tests. Right now those tests are red because the fixture no longer satisfies the parser’s checksum contract.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Stamp the synthetic root-tree block checksum after finalizing the leaf contents, and confirm the minimal open/root-tree tests go green independently of the larger readdir fixture family.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:27.513003227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:01.840021095Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:23:01.838087183Z","close_reason":"checksum stamping added in commit 2b7aba8; remaining NotFound error is separate issue (missing default subvolume ROOT_ITEM)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-dm5m","title":"Add ext4 inline-data adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback /testing-fuzzing round: add deterministic adversarial coverage for ext4 inline-data inode/xattr-body parser boundaries so fuzz-relevant malformed seeds are regression-tested without requiring a live fuzz campaign.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:46:39.650249983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:54:48.367744967Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:54:48.365563188Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inline-data adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fuzz"]}
 {"id":"bd-dn1hf","title":"[HIGH] ffs-core btrfs root-tree and readdir tests now fail on CRC32C checksum mismatch","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` currently fails 10 `ffs-core` tests that all unwrap the same parser error: `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Failed tests
- `tests::open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`
- `tests::walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Failure sites
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22693`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22711`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22723`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22736`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23342`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23365`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23388`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23409`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23442`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23489`

## Why this is high severity
This is a workspace-red regression in core synthetic btrfs coverage. It breaks basic `OpenFs::from_device()` and `readdir()` expectations for internal fixtures, so new btrfs behavior cannot be trusted until the checksum stamping / parser-validation mismatch is resolved.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Audit recent btrfs fixture builders or validation-path changes so synthetic root-tree and dir-index blocks have checksums stamped consistently with current parser verification.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

+{"id":"bd-o5114","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_image fixture omits default-root ROOT_ITEM for OpenOptions::default()","description":"# Review finding

`cargo test --workspace --all-targets` is still red in the basic btrfs smoke suite even after checksum stamping landed. The remaining failure shape is no longer CRC mismatch; it is `NotFound("btrfs ROOT_ITEM for default_root `default` (objectid 5)")` from `OpenFs::from_device(..., OpenOptions::default())`.

## Failed tests
- `tests::open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`

## Evidence
- Fixture builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22119-22204`
- It currently emits a single synthetic root-tree leaf entry with `objectid=256`, `item_type=ROOT_ITEM`, and 8 bytes of dummy payload.
- Current selection logic resolves `BtrfsMountSelection::DefaultRoot` to `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` (`objectid 5`) and requires a matching `ROOT_ITEM` before it can fall back to the superblock `root_dir_objectid`: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2395-2428`.
- Workspace repro still fails these four tests with the `NotFound` error after the checksum-only fix.

## Why this is high severity
This keeps the full workspace test gate red in the most basic btrfs `OpenFs` smoke path. It is a separate regression from the already-filed readdir checksum family.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Make `build_btrfs_image()` model the current default-root mount contract: include a `ROOT_ITEM` for `BTRFS_FS_TREE_OBJECTID` (objectid 5) with parseable enough payload for default-root resolution, or explicitly adjust the smoke tests to open a non-default root selection if that is the intended contract. Verify the four basic open/root-tree tests independently once fixed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:41.432054034Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:41.432054034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-u2g7m","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-core mounted-image byte-corruption recovery harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-core` open/mount recovery boundary with raw byte corruption as the driver. The harness should focus on repair/recover paths instead of only parser entry points.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2132-2387` mount/open path
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2755-2929` ext4 crash/orphan recovery
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2204-2235` btrfs tree-log recovery branch

Coverage goals:
- random byte corruption in superblocks, root-tree blocks, orphan links, log roots, and recovery flags
- deterministic classification into `FfsError`, safe replay skip, or successful mount
- no panic, no hang, no unbounded traversal during corrupted-image recovery

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- shared corpus seeded from current clean/dirty ext4 and btrfs recovery fixtures
- crashers minimized and promoted to regressions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:18:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-core","fuzzing","mount-path","recovery","repair","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-u355g","title":"[HIGH] open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree smoke assertions are stale against default-root mount semantics","description":"# Review finding

The `build_btrfs_image()` smoke suite is failing early today on missing default-root resolution, but the test body behind `open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree` is also stale against the current mount contract. It still asserts that the walked root tree contains exactly one item with `objectid=256` and an 8-byte dummy payload.

## Evidence
- Failing suite today: `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree` is in the red set from `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`, but it currently aborts before its assertions because `OpenFs::from_device(..., OpenOptions::default())` cannot resolve a default-root `ROOT_ITEM`.
- Test assertions: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22736-22748` expect `items.len() == 1`, `items[0].key.objectid == 256`, `item_type == 132`, and dummy payload bytes.
- Current selection logic opens btrfs under `BtrfsMountSelection::DefaultRoot` when no selector is supplied: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2395-2428`. Any fixture fix that adds the missing default-root `ROOT_ITEM` for objectid 5 or otherwise models current semantics will likely invalidate the old single-item/objectid-256 expectation unless the test explicitly opens a non-default selection.

## Why this is high severity
This is a likely second workspace-red failure masked by the earlier `NotFound` abort. If the fixture is repaired without aligning this test, the gate will still stay red.

## Repro
Current gate: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Either:
- make the smoke test open an explicit non-default/root-tree-focused selection and keep the current objectid-256 assertions, or
- update the fixture and assertions to reflect the default-root mount contract and the resulting walked root-tree inventory.
Do not stop at making `OpenFs::from_device()` succeed; prove the post-open root-tree assertions still match the intended contract.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:34.746033272Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:34.746033272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","tests","workspace-gate"]}
